definition: AI productivity gains of 0.5-1.0pp sustained would be the single biggest factor that could invalidate the fiscal dominance thesis by improving the r-g differential. No historical precedent exists for technology-driven fiscal resolution at this speed. Post-WWII productivity boom took 15-20 years to fully manifest. BLS data currently shows no aggregate TFP structural break. Probability estimates span 5-15%, confirming unknowability.
evidence: Universal agreement across agents that this is the key wildcard
Post-WWII productivity boom took 15-20 years to fully manifest
BLS: no aggregate TFP structural break currently observable (KB: no_aggregate_tfp_breakthrough confidence 9)
Probability estimates span an order of magnitude (5% generalist_02 to 10-15% macro_strategist_01)
Knowledge base devotes more analytical investment to doom scenario than escape scenario (structural bias)
open_questions: Can AI productivity manifest within the relevant 3-7 year fiscal dominance horizon?
Would sector-specific AI productivity gains translate to aggregate TFP improvement?
Does the knowledge base have sufficient analytical investment in the escape scenario?
What early indicators would signal AI-driven TFP acceleration?
